---
title: Sample Spaces and Events
description: Probability starts with possible outcomes.
---

::: {.learning-objectives}
### Learning Objectives

- Define sample space.
- Define event.
- Compute simple probabilities.
:::

The sample space is the set of all possible outcomes. An event is a subset of
that space.

::: {.practice-box}
1. What is the sample space for one coin flip?
2. What event means "getting heads"?
3. What is the probability of rolling a 6 on a fair die?
:::

## Solutions

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## Solution 1

Answer: `{H, T}`.

The two possible outcomes are heads and tails.
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## Solution 2

Answer: `{H}`.

The event is the subset of the sample space where heads occurs.
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## Solution 3

Answer: `1/6`.

A fair die has six equally likely outcomes, and only one of them is `6`.
:::
